title: Mixed Vegetable Soup with Roti
description: A chunky mixed-veg soup with everyday Indian seasoning, served with rotis for a light but complete dinner plate.
explanation: Soup alone can feel incomplete in an Indian diet plan; pairing it with roti makes a practical evening main that still centers vegetables.
Keep the vegetables chunky rather than pureed so the bowl eats like a meal. Onion and garlic build the base in the standard version.
ingredients: 4 cups Water or light vegetable stock
instructions: Heat oil, crackle cumin, saute onion until soft. Add ginger-garlic and tomato; cook 2 minutes.
Add all vegetables, turmeric, salt, and pepper. Stir 1–2 minutes.
Pour in water or stock. Bring to a boil, then simmer until vegetables are tender but not mushy, 12–15 minutes.
Adjust consistency: slightly brothy for a lighter dinner, thicker if you prefer stew-like texture.
Finish with lemon and coriander. Serve hot with rotis for dipping or on the side.
stepNotes: Cut vegetables to similar size so they cook evenly.
Avoid over-blending; texture is part of satiety.
A leftover roti torn into the soup turns it into a quick one-bowl meal.
Pepper and lemon matter more than heavy chilli for a clean finish.
Salt near the end if your stock is already seasoned.

variationSections.title: Vegetable mixes
items: Add bottle gourd for a milder, lighter soup.

variationSections.title: Plate adjustments
items: Millet roti for a gluten-free variation.
Stir in cooked moong dal for more protein.
Skip onion and garlic and use hing for a no-onion/no-garlic variation (not the default).
